1) Requires valid URLs that are accessible and contain text content.

3) Performance depends on the quality and relevance of the website content. 
4) May return incomplete or inaccurate information if the website content is not comprehensive. 
5) Limited by the chunking and embedding process which may miss context. 
6) Requires OpenAI API access for embeddings and LLM generation.

6) Use it as part of a multi-step research process rather than a single source of truth.

result:
The Congo River has a length of approximately 4,700 km (2,900 miles).[1](https://www.britannica.com/place/Congo-River), [2](https://study.com/academy/lesson/congo-river-basin-climate-physical-features.html) Some sources state its length as 4,374 km (2,718 miles)[3](https://dlab.epfl.ch/wikispeedia/wpcd/wp/c/Congo_River.htm) or 4,370 km (2,720 miles) when measured with its main tributary, the Lualaba.[4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congo_River) Other measurements indicate lengths of 4,667 km (2,900 mi)[3](https://dlab.epfl.ch/wikispeedia/wpcd/wp/c/Congo_River.htm) or 4,344 km (2,700 miles).[5](https://www.ebsco.com/research-starters/history/congo-river)

When considering the Congo–Lualaba–Luvua–Luapula–Chambeshi River system, its overall length is about 4,700 km (2,900 mi), making it the world's ninth-longest river. It is t[4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congo_River)he second-longest river in Africa, after the Nile. The Con[3](https://dlab.epfl.ch/wikispeedia/wpcd/wp/c/Congo_River.htm), [1](https://www.britannica.com/place/Congo-River), [5](https://www.ebsco.com/research-starters/history/congo-river), [2](https://study.com/academy/lesson/congo-river-basin-climate-physical-features.html)go River is also notable for being the world's deepest recorded river, with depths reaching around 220 meters (720 ft). It cros[4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congo_River), [2](https://study.com/academy/lesson/congo-river-basin-climate-physical-features.html)ses the equator twice.[4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congo_River), [5](https://www.ebsco.com/research-starters/history/congo-river)

direct_output:
**Process Summary:** 

1. **Initial Analysis:** The query asks for the length of the second deepest river in the world.
2. **Action Step 1:** Used the Wikipedia_RAG_Search_Tool to search for information about the second deepest river in the world, including its length. The tool returned information about the Congo River, which is the second-longest but not the second-deepest river.
3. **Action Step 2:** Found the length of the Congo River to be approximately 4,700 km (2,900 miles).
4. **Action Step 3:** Used the Wikipedia_RAG_Search_Tool again to find the name and length of the second deepest river in the world. The tool returned information about the Tekezé River, which is the second-deepest river in the world.
5. **Action Step 4:** Found that the Tekezé River is located in Ethiopia and is a tributary of the Nile. Further searches for the length of the Tekezé River provided no specific measurement, indicating that reliable length data might not be available.
6. **Action Step 5:** Noted that the depth of the Congo River is around 220 meters (720 ft), but this information was not directly relevant to the query about the second-deepest river’s length.

**Answer:** The length of the second deepest river in the world, the Tekezé River, is not reliably documented, but it is known to be a tributary of the Nile in Ethiopia.
